original: English chemistJoseph Priestleydiscovers oxygen by isolating it in its gaseous state
Mariner 6 sends close-up photos of Mars
Nuclear-powered submarine USS Nautilus begins the first transit of the North Pole in Operation Sunshine
Caroline Herschel becomes the first woman to discover a comet
Harriet Quimbypasses her pilot's test and becomes the first US woman to earn an Aero Club of America aviator's certificate
Dan "Big Daddy" Garlits becomes the first drag racer to run the 1/4 mile at over 200 mph (201.34 mph [324 km/h] at Island Dragway, New Jersey)
Ex-MLB outfielder Glen Gorbous throws a regulation baseball a record 136 m (445 ft and 10 in) in a game promotion
Anne Frank's last diary entry; she is arrested 3 days later
Black people vote for the first time in a US state election in the South (Tennessee)
First commercial building heated by the Sun (Albuquerque, New Mexico)

Henrietta Lacks [Loretta Pleasant], African-American tobacco farmer whose cancer cells are the source of the HeLa cell line, extensively used in medical research since the 1950s (1st immortalised cell line), born in Roanoke, Virginia (d. 1951)
First microgravity research begins
Francis Scott Key, American lawyer, poet and composer of the lyrics to "Star-Spangled Banner", born in Carroll County, Maryland (d. 1843)
Adam Clayton Powell Jr.is elected as the first African American congressman from New York
Nate Colbert ties the MLB record by hitting five home runs and sets a record with 13 RBIs as the Padres beat Atlanta 9-0 and 11-7 in a doubleheader
Reigning world F1 championNiki Laudaof Austria suffers a near-fatal crash during the German Grand Prix at the Nürburgring motorsports complex, Nürburg, Germany
The first Jeep is produced
Slavery Abolition Act 1833 comes into effect, abolishing slavery across the British Empire
France becomes the first country to use the metric system

George Harrison's benefit concerts for Bangladesh (two shows) take place at Madison Square Garden, NYC; performers includeBob Dylan,Ringo Starr,Ravi Shankar,Eric Clapton,Billy Preston, and Leon Russell
Boston Red Sox pitcher Bill Monbouquette no-hits the Chicago White Sox in a 1-0 win at White Sox Park
Gerd Wessig of East Germany becomes the first male to set a high jump world record of 2.36 m (7 ft 9 in) at the Moscow Olympics
Richard Pettywins the Dixie 500 in Atlanta, Georgia, to become the first NASCAR driver to win $1 million in career earnings
Babe Ruthhits home run #42 in the Yankees' 12-1 win vs. the St. Louis Browns, putting him 4 weeks ahead of his 1927 pace
Maria Mitchell, 1st US woman astronomer (Vassar), born in Nantucket, Massachusetts (d. 1889)
Battle of the Nile: British Royal Navy under AdmiralHoratio Nelsonattacks and decimates the French fleet at Aboukir Bay off the Nile Delta, Egypt
Scottish Lotus driverJim Clarkwins the German Grand Prix at the Nürburgring to clinch his second F1 World Championship

Frank Grant, American Baseball HOF 2nd baseman (International League; pioneer early Negro leagues; considered greatest African-American player of 19th century), born in Pittsfield, Massachusetts (d. 1937)
First Scout camp opens on Brownsea Island in Poole Harbour, Dorset
Beatles' single "A Hard Day's Night" goes to #1 and stays at #1 for 2 weeks
"Endless Love" single released byDiana RossandLionel Richie(Billboard Song of the Year 1981, Billboard Greatest Song Duet of All-Time)
Influential country music group theCarter Familymakes its first recording with producer Ralph Peer in Bristol, Tennessee
Georges Charpak, Polish-French physicist who invented and developed subatomic particle detectors (Nobel 1992), born in Paris, France (d. 2010)
Jens Juul Holst, Danish physician and physiologist known for discovering GLP-1 (2025 Breakthrough Prize), born in  Copenhagen, Denmark
Walther Gerlach, German physicist who, with Otto Stern, co-discovered spin quantization in a magnetic field (Stern–Gerlach effect), born in Biebrich, Rhineland Palatinate, Germany (d. 1979)
John Friend Mahoney, American physician who pioneered the treatment of syphilis with penicillin, born in Fond du Lac, Wisconsin (d. 1957)
Benjamin Mays, African-American educator (Morehouse, Howard University) and civil rights leader, born in Ninety Six, South Carolina (d. 1984)
Gerda Taro (Gerta Pohorylle), German-born war photojournalist who covered the Spanish Civil War, born in Stuttgart, Germany (d. 1937)
Jackie Ormes, African-American cartoonist (Torchy Brown), born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania (d. 1985)
Geoffrey Holder, Trinidadian-American actor, choreographer, singer, director (Annie; The Wiz), and painter, born in Port of Spain, Trinidad and Tobago (d. 2014)
Seán Ó Riada [John Reidy], Irish composer (Mise Éire), traditional music revivalist, and bandleader (Ceoltóirí Chualann), born in Cork, County Cork, Ireland (d. 1971)
Fiona Stanley, Australian epidemiologist (Telethon Institute for Child Health Research), born in Little Bay, Sydney, Australia
Richard O. Covey, American astronaut (STS 51I, 26, 38, 61) and USAF officer, born in Fayetteville, Arkansas
Yury Romanenko, Soviet cosmonaut (Soyuz 26, 38, TM-2), born in Koltubanka, Russia
Pancho Villa [Francisco Guilledo], Filipino world boxing champion, born in Ilog, Negros Occidental, Philippine Islands (d. 1925)
Dom DeLuise, American comic actor (Mel Brooks films Dean Martin Show; Cannonball Run; Fatso; Don Bluth films), director, and cookbook writer, born in Brooklyn, New York City (d. 2009)
Cliff Branch, American Pro Football HOF wide receiver (Super Bowl 1976, 80, 84 Oakland/LA Raiders; 3 × First-team All-Pro; 4 × Pro Bowl), born in Houston, Texas (d. 2019)
Robert Cray, American Grammy Award-winning blues guitarist, and singer ("Smoking Gun"; "The Forecast Calls for Pain"), born in Columbus, Georgia
Paddy Moloney, Irish uilleann pipe and tin whistle player, composer, and producer (The Chieftains), born in Donnycarney, County Dublin, Ireland (d. 2021)
Frank Worrell, West Indian cricket batsman and captain (51 Tests @ 49.48; 9 x 100, HS 261; Barbados, Jamaica), born in Bridgetown, Saint Michael, Barbados (d. 1967)
Lionel Bart [Begleiter], British pop music composer and writer (Oliver!), born in London (d. 1999)
Meena Kumari, Indian film actress (Parineeta), born in Dadar, Mumbai, India (d. 1972)
Brian Fagan, British archaeologist and writer (The Great Warming: Climate Change and the Rise and Fall of Civilizations), born in Birmingham, England (d. 2025)
Co Hoedeman, Dutch-Canadian animator and director (The Sand Castle, Oddball), born in Amsterdam, Netherlands (d. 2025)[1]
George de Hevesy, Hungarian radiochemist (Nobel Prize in Chemistry -1943), born in Budapest, Austria-Hungary (d. 1966)[1]
Ottavio Bottecchia, Italian cyclist (the first Italian winner of the Tour de France), born in San Martino di Colle Umberto, Italy (d. 1927)

Ilya Ivanovich Ivanov, Russian biologist who perfected the artificial insemination of animals and showed that his technology allowed one stallion to fertilize up to 500 mares, born in Shchigry, Kursk Oblast, Russia (d. 1932)

Morris Stoloff, American violinist, Academy Award-winning composer, music director (Columbia Pictures, 1938-62), and orchestra leader (Moonglow/Theme from Picnic), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 1980)
Maria Cole [née Hawkins], American jazz singer and wife of Nat King Cole, born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 2012)
Yves Saint Laurent, French fashion designer, born in Oran, French Algeria (d. 2008)
